summary: crypto-credential-scanner ships wallet/credential stealer with hardcoded C2
details: The package contains three modules that together implement a credential and wallet harvester with a hardcoded callback endpoint. wallet-scanner.js performs filesystem enumeration, executes shell commands (curl, ping), and POSTs collected data over HTTPS. scanner.js collects host identity (os.hostname(), execSync('whoami'), username/cwd fields) and POSTs it to a hardcoded GitHub Pages endpoint at https://ddjidd564.github.io (referenced from scanner.js lines 161 and 323). build-verifier.js similarly combines fs/os/https/child_process with POST calls and an execSync('docker push...') invocation, packaging hostname/username/cwd into outbound requests. The package's name claims a defensive/scanning purpose, but the structural shape — wallet enumeration, host fingerprint collection, hardcoded attacker-controlled exfiltration endpoint, and shell-out primitives — is a credential/wallet stealer. The GitHub Pages domain `ddjidd564.github.io` is an anonymous, attacker-controlled static-hosting endpoint inconsistent with any legitimate publisher infrastructure.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
